page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled via a 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity delivers an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The system runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top 10% tier. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations both rank in the top 25% tier, reaching 296.76 GB/sec and 130.55 GB/sec respectively. Computational performance is solid, with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores in the top 25% tier. Database operations also perform strongly, ranking in the top 25%. However, the instance exhibits limitations in large language model (LLM) text generation, with performance falling into the bottom 10% for both small and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gd.4xlarge offers a high-density memory profile combined with fast local NVMe storage, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that require low-latency scratch space without the overhead of external network storage. Given the hardware specifications and benchmark results, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, data buffering, and high-throughput decompression tasks. It is less suitable for heavy LLM text generation workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 16 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and strong large-block read/write memory bandwidth in the top 25%. Computational benchmarks like Geekbench and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 25%, as do database operations. However, LLM text generation performance is weak to poor. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing workloads requiring high-speed local storage, offering strong resource density for memory-bound applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 16 cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD local drive.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring high-density RAM and fast local NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
128 GB of system memory providing 8 GB per core
Bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
